sql_array: CREATE TABLE test_data (id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY, name VARCHAR, timestamp_column TIMESTAMP, interval_column INTERVAL);
INSERT INTO test_data (id, name, timestamp_column, interval_column) VALUES (1, '', TIMESTAMP '2023-10-22 10:30:00', INTERVAL '1 DAY');
INSERT INTO test_data (id, name, timestamp_column, interval_column) VALUES (2, 'test', TIMESTAMP '2023-10-22 10:30:00', INTERVAL '5 DAY');
SELECT name, printf('Timestamp shifted by %s is %s', interval_column, timestamp_column + interval_column) AS shifted_timestamp FROM test_data WHERE id = 1;
SELECT id, printf('Formatted Result: %s with ID %d', name, id) AS formatted_id FROM test_data WHERE name IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, printf('The result of adding 3 days to %s is %s', name, timestamp_column + INTERVAL '3 DAY') AS added_days FROM test_data WHERE timestamp_column IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, printf('The Final Output for entry %d: %s at %s', id, name, (timestamp_column + interval_column) IS NOT NULL) AS final_output FROM test_data;
SELECT id, COUNT(*) OVER (PARTITION BY printf('%s', name)) AS name_count FROM test_data;
CREATE TABLE additional_data (ref_id INTEGER, value DOUBLE);
INSERT INTO additional_data (ref_id, value) VALUES (1, 100.5), (2, NULL), (1, 200.0);

SELECT a.name, SUM(b.value) AS total_value, printf('Total for %s: %d', a.name, SUM(b.value)) AS formatted_sum FROM test_data a LEFT JOIN additional_data b ON a.id = b.ref_id GROUP BY a.name HAVING total_value IS NOT NULL;
SELECT id, printf('Total timestamp addition for %s over 7 days is %s', name, timestamp_column + INTERVAL '7 DAY') AS seven_days_later FROM test_data WHERE name != '';
SELECT name, printf('Value: %s', COALESCE(NULLIF(name, ''), 'N/A')) AS safe_name FROM test_data;

SELECT id, timestamp_column, CASE WHEN interval_column IS NOT NULL THEN printf('Final timestamp is %s', timestamp_column + interval_column) ELSE 'NULL case' END AS processed_timestamp FROM test_data WHERE name IS NULL;
